THE PARTIES STIPULATED THAT ANY OTHER COMPLAINTS FILED OR TO BE FILED BY BRAYTON PLAINTIFFS AGAINST LEHIGH HANSON IN ASBESTOS CASES SHALL BE GOVERNED BY THE RULING ON THIS MOTION HEARD ON 9/30/2011. PAILLET V. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. (CASE NUMBER CGC-11-275807) WAS DESIGNATED AS THE LEAD CASE. AN ORDER PURSUANT TO THE STIPULATION WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Victor Paillet
Camille K. Fong of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-362-2580) for defendants / opposing parties Moore securities Company, alleged alternate entity of Moore Dry Dock and Moore Dry Dock Company
Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to sign in sheet for other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE

Hearing on Plaintiff's motion for order maintaining current trial date with preference was held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference pursuant to CCP 36(a) is granted. Trial date is September 23, 2013. Last day for preference is October 9, 2013. As to the discovery issues: (1) Berry & Berry will subpoena the records from Kaiser. Parties to meet and confer to resolve any disputes relating to plaintiff's current medical condition. (2) Berry & Berry can pursue any medicare records requested before the discovery cutoff date. (3) The defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Judin, Esq., of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0754), appearing for defendant / moving party Lehigh Hanson, Inc.
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq., and Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Victor Paillet

RULING:
DEFENDANT LEHIGH HANSON, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.'s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff(s)'s request for jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiff(s) failed to make any showing of the second element of the alter ego doctrine - i.e. "the facts are such that an adherence to the fiction of the separate existence of the corporation would, under the particular circumstances, sanction a fraud or promote injustice" (9 Witkin, Summary of California Law (10th ed. 2005) "Corporations" 10, quoting Minifie v. Rowley (1921) 187 Cal. 481, 487)) - or that any of the jurisdictional discovery sought to be taken will produce evidence to prove that element. (See also Sonora Diamond Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 523, 539 (alter ego doctrine not a grounds to impose personal jurisdiction where there was no evidence of any wrongdoing by either the subsidiary or the parent or any evidence of injustice flowing from the recognition of the subsidiary's separate corporate identity)). The most recent supplemental papers filed by plaintiff(s) merely show that plaintiffs, if entitled to do so under Civil Code section 3295, desire to take punitive damages discovery from Lehigh Hanson, not that any fraud or injustice would result from adhering to the fictional separate existence of Lehigh Hanson from Kaiser Gypsum. Plaintiff(s)'s desire to take punitive damages discovery from Lehigh Hanson can be fully accomplished through means other than invoking the alter ego doctrine. The parties stipulated that any other complaints filed or to be filed by Brayton Plaintiffs against Lehigh Hanson in Asbestos cases shall be governed by the ruling on this motion heard on 9/30/2011. Paillet v. Crown Cork & Seal Company, Inc. (Case Number CGC-11-275807) was designated as the lead case. An order pursuant to the stipulation was signed in open court. Counsel for defendant is to prepare an order regarding the motion to quash. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
